extraction.one_line_summary: QUINTANA Nairo attacked multiple times on the final climb, dropped THOMAS with 3km to go after repeated accelerations, caught and dropped ŠPILAK who attacked earlier
tactical_patterns: QUINTANA launched repeated accelerations to isolate THOMAS from teammates YATES and URAN who couldn't respond to surges
ŠPILAK attacked three times early on climb to gain isolation, but this strategy left him exposed when caught
THOMAS deliberately dropped into tempo mode when seeing YATES/QUINTANA/URAN together, controlling through pacing rather than matching surges
Headwind on 8% gradient made sitting wheel more valuable than typical, rewarding QUINTANA's surge-and-wait tactic over sustained efforts

rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: showed unprecedented early-season form, climbing dominance suggests Giro potential on queen stages with repeated accelerations
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: strong GC development, controlled group through tempo riding but lacks explosive response to multiple accelerations
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: couldn't follow THOMAS when he bridged to QUINTANA, exposed gap between British teammates on steep climbs
rider_signals.ŠPILAK Simon: negative: three early accelerations exhausted him before summit, couldn't sustain effort when caught by fresh QUINTANA
rider_signals.URAN Rigoberto: neutral: content to sit on wheels rather than contribute, tactical waiting game failed when pace ramped
